courses.name: Survey of Greek Civilization
courses.description: Introduction to the study of Greek antiquity and the discipline of Classics and its methodologies. Greek culture and society are set in their historical contexts and studied through readings from representative ancient authors (in English translation) and through the art and architecture of the period.

courses.name: Survey of Roman Civilization
courses.description: Introduction to the study of Roman antiquity and the discipline of Classics and its methodologies. The culture and society are set in their historical context and studied through readings from representative ancient authors (in English translation) and through the art and architecture of the period.

courses.name: Elementary Language Tutorial I
courses.description: Elementary study of an ancient language.

courses.name: Elementary Language Tutorial II
courses.description: Elementary study of an ancient language.

courses.name: Introduction to Archaeology I
courses.description: Introduction to the history, theory and practice of field archaeology.  Excavations from all time periods and global regions will be discussed.  Focus will be placed on excavation methods and technology, including dating, that enhance understanding of sites both on land and underwater.

courses.name: Introduction to Archaeology II
courses.description: Continues the examination of various aspects of field archaeology begun in .  This course places greater focus on recent approaches to the interpretation of remains.  These include environmental, cognitive and bioarchaeological approaches.

courses.name: Intermediate Language Tutorial I
courses.description: Intermediate study of an ancient language.

courses.name: Intermediate Language Tutorial II
courses.description: Intermediate study of an ancient language.

courses.name: Greek and Roman Epic
courses.description: An examination of the genre of epic in Greco-Roman antiquity, including a close reading of translations of Homer and Vergil.

courses.name: Greek and Roman Drama
courses.description: An examination of the genres of tragedy and comedy in Greco-Roman antiquity.

courses.name: Scientific and Medical Terminology
courses.description: Examination of Ancient Greek and Latin roots of technical terms found in the sciences, engineering, and medicine.

courses.name: Greek Religion
courses.description: A study of religion in ancient Greece.

courses.name: Roman Religion
courses.description: A study of religion in ancient Rome.

courses.name: Ancient Philosophy: The Search for Wisdom
courses.description: An exploration of ancient philosophy as a search for wisdom and happiness from its Presocratic beginnings in Greece to its development in the Hellenistic world and Imperial Rome. Emphasis on philosophy as a contemplative activity and as a way of life.

courses.name: Greek Art and Archaeology
courses.description: The art, architecture and archaeology of ancient Greece.  Vase painting, sculpture, architecture, town planning and analogous arts.

courses.name: Roman Art and Archaeology
courses.description: The art, architecture and archaeology of the ancient Romans.  Vase painting, sculpture, architecture, town planning and analogous arts are studied.

courses.name: Ancient Science and Technology
courses.description: The development and application of ancient science and technology in the fields of ancient engineering, machinery, metallurgy, transport, building, agriculture and Hippocratic medicine; the social position of craftsmen and artisans, the attitude of intellectuals to science and manual labour, the effects of slavery.

courses.name: Classical Mythology
courses.description: A study of classical mythology, emphasizing its use in Greek and Roman literature and its place in classical art and religion. There is some discussion of classical myths in terms of contemporary interpretations of myth.

courses.name: Origins of the Greeks
courses.description: The history of ancient Greece from the Bronze Age through the Archaic period.

courses.name: Democracy to Alexander
courses.description: The history of ancient Greece from the classical period to Alexander.

courses.name: Rise of the Roman Empire
courses.description: The history of ancient Rome from early Rome to the end of the Republic.

courses.name: Rome of the Caesars
courses.description: The history of ancient Rome from the end of the Republic to the coming of Islam.

courses.name: Studies in Classical Civilization
courses.description: A study of a selected topic in ancient history, literature, languages, culture, archaeology and/or technology.

courses.name: Topics in Ancient History
courses.description: A study of a selected topic in ancient history.

courses.name: Early Greek Philosophy
courses.description: A study of the pre-Socratic philosophers and of the Sophists and Socrates.

courses.name: Topics in Classical Civilization
courses.description: A study of a selected topic in classical civilization.

courses.name: The Later Roman Empire
courses.description: The study of major developments - administrative, ecclesiastical, cultural and societal - of the later Roman Empire.

courses.name: Topics in Ancient Philosophy
courses.description: A study of philosophers, texts, problems and issues in ancient philosophy, generally with a focus on Plato and Aristotle.

courses.name: Studies in Greek History
courses.description: Study of a period or theme in Greek History.

courses.name: Studies in Roman History
courses.description: Study of a period or theme in Roman History.

courses.name: Field Work I: Greek and Roman World
courses.description: Students will participate for a minimum of three weeks on an archaeological field project (i.e., excavation or survey) relevant to the Greek and Roman world.  They will learn archaeological documentation and the analysis, recording, and processing of finds.

courses.name: Studies in Greek Art
courses.description: A study of period or theme in the art and archaeology of Ancient Greece. Topics may vary from year to year.

courses.name: Studies in Roman Art
courses.description: A study of a period or theme in the art and archaeology of the ancient Romans.  Topics may vary from year to year.

courses.name: Greek and Roman Studies Abroad
courses.description: This course combines academic study in Canada with first hand examination of museum collections and sites of the ancient world, normally in Greece and Italy.  Course content varies from year to year.

courses.name: Studies in Greek Literature
courses.description: A study of an author or topic in Greek literature. Contents of this course vary from year to year.

courses.name: Studies in Roman Literature
courses.description: A study of an author or topic in Roman literature.

courses.name: Field Work II: Greek and Roman World
courses.description: Students participate for a minimum of three weeks in a position of responsibility (for example, as a trench supervisor or lab assistant) on an archaeological field project relevant to the Greek and Roman world.

courses.name: Topics in Ancient History
courses.description: Intended for Honours students in History and Classics who should normally be in the third and fourth-years.

courses.name: Seminar in Greek and Roman Studies
Seminar in Greek and Roman Studies
Directed Readings and Research
courses.description: These courses consist of supervised readings and research projects in a specific area of Classical Civilization to be chosen in consultation with  a faculty Supervisor who agrees to oversee a student's proposed research.

courses.name: Introduction to Greek I
courses.description: A course for beginners in ancient Greek, designed to give students a grasp of basic grammatical forms and vocabulary (with reference to English derivatives) through the reading of continuous Greek.

courses.name: Introduction to Greek II
courses.description: A course for students with some previous knowledge of the language: study of grammatical forms and constructions; acquisition of reading skills.

courses.name: Intermediate Greek I
courses.description: Further study of the language; introduction to the reading of ancient Greek authors.

courses.name: Intermediate Greek II
courses.description: Continued study of the language; reading of selected prose and poetry by ancient Greek authors; development of translation skills.

courses.name: Advanced Greek I
courses.description: Reading and critical discussion of selections from ancient Greek.

courses.name: Advanced Greek II
courses.description: Reading and critical discussion of selections from ancient Greek.

courses.name: Introduction to Latin I
courses.description: A course for beginners in Latin, designed to give students a grasp of basic grammatical forms and vocabulary (with reference to English derivatives) through the reading of continuous Latin.

courses.name: Introduction to Latin II
courses.description: A course for students with some previous knowledge of the language: study of grammatical forms and constructions; acquisition of reading skills.

courses.name: Intermediate Latin I
courses.description: Further study of the language; introduction to the reading of Latin authors.

courses.name: Intermediate Latin II
courses.description: Continued study of the language; reading of selected prose and poetry by Latin authors; development of translation skills.

courses.name: Advanced Latin I
courses.description: Reading and critical discussion of selections from Latin poetry.

courses.name: Advanced Latin II
courses.description: Reading and critical discussion of selections from Latin prose.

free_electives.degree: Greek and Roman Studies B.A. Honours (20.0 credits)
Greek and Roman Studies B.A. Combined Honours (20.0 credits)
free_electives.text: 7.  Sufficient free electives to make 20.0 credits total for the degree.

free_electives.degree: Greek and Roman Studies B.A. (15.0 credits)
